title: Oxybaphus melanotrichus
taxonomy: Oxybaphus melanotrichus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This plant is native to Mexico and is often found in dry, rocky areas. It is a type of annual or short-lived perennial that produces showy white flowers. The classification of Oxybaphus melanotrichus is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its hairy stems and leaves. Its classification within the family Polemoniaceae places it among other flowering plants that share similar characteristics.

intro: Oxybaphus melanotrichus, commonly known as black-hairy four o'clock, is a flowering plant species native to Mexico and Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to the relief of symptoms associated with digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Oxybaphus melanotrichus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
